HTML.BlockWrapper TYPE: string VERSION: 1.3.0 DEFAULT: 'p' --DESCRIPTION-- <p> String name of element to wrap inline elements that are inside a block context. This only occurs in the children of blockquote in strict mode. </p> <p> Example: by default value, <code>&lt;blockquote&gt;Foo&lt;/blockquote&gt;</code> would become <code>&lt;blockquote&gt;&lt;p&gt;Foo&lt;/p&gt;&lt;/blockquote&gt;</code>. The <code>&lt;p&gt;</code> tags can be replaced with whatever you desire, as long as it is a block level element. </p> --# vim: et sw=4 sts=4
